rationale: The citation is used in the paper's core theorizing, not as a passing background mention. The authors explicitly rely on Thompson, Hamilton, and Rust (2005) to justify the key mechanism that more attributes can function as a cue of usefulness, which underpins their hypotheses and mediation logic. Even without direct method reuse, this prior work materially supports the conceptual setup of the citing paper.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Thompson, Hamilton, and Rust (2005) as a conceptual basis for the claim that attribute numerosity signals usefulness/capability. It then builds its own theory and tests around how that usefulness cue differentially shifts hedonic versus utilitarian choice.
